label: whatitdoes
out:
WHAT IT DOES (the automated path — the default)
    Scheduled every few minutes, it ATTACHES to your already-open classic
    Outlook, reads the study mail folder(s) you point it at, and files EVERY new
    message as one searchable HTML note — then rebuilds index.html. You do not
    drag anything. It is incremental and idempotent: each message is filed once
    (tracked by Outlook EntryID in .state\seen.json) and your email is NEVER
    moved, deleted, or modified — it only reads.

label: attach
sanitize_code
out:
function Sanitize-Html([string]$h){
  if (-not $h){ return $h }
  $h = $h -replace '(?is)<script.*?</script>',''
  $h = $h -replace '(?is)<style.*?</style>',''
  $h = $h -replace '(?is)<(iframe|object|embed).*?</\1>',''
  $h = $h -replace '(?i)<(script|style|iframe|object|embed|link|meta|base)\b[^>]*>',''
  $h = $h -replace '(?i)\son\w+\s*=\s*("[^"]*"|''[^'']*''|[^\s>]+)',''   # on*= event handlers
  $h = $h -replace '(?i)(src|background)\s*=\s*("|'')\s*https?:','$1=$2blocked-remote:'  # stop remote fetches
  $h = $h -replace '(?i)javascript:','blocked:'
  return $h

label: proof_src
cmd: Write-Host 'the source email carried:'; '  <script> present : ' + [bool]((Get-Content -Raw .\_Inbox\_processed\dta03.html) -match '<script'); '  remote pixel    : ' + [regex]::Match((Get-Content -Raw .\_Inbox\_processed\dta03.html),'https://tracker[^"]+').Value
out:
the source email carried:
  <script> present : True
  remote pixel    : https://tracker.example.com/pixel.gif?u=open

label: proof_note
cmd: Write-Host 'the filed note carries:'; '  <script> present : ' + [bool]((Get-Content -Raw ".\Notes\20260622_091400__CP-101_-_Data_transfer_DTA-03_received__640e411e.html") -match '<script'); '  remote pixel    : ' + [regex]::Match((Get-Content -Raw ".\Notes\20260622_091400__CP-101_-_Data_transfer_DTA-03_received__640e411e.html"),'blocked-remote://tracker[^"]+').Value
out:
the filed note carries:
  <script> present : False
  remote pixel    : blocked-remote://tracker.example.com/pixel.gif?u=open

label: vba
out:
' This is the AD-HOC one-click for the rare email that lives outside your study
' folder. The routine path is automatic: Update-StudyKB.ps1 reads the study
' folder on a schedule (-StudyFolders) — you don't click anything. Use this only
' to capture a stray forwarded thread into the KB inbox in one click.
'
' Outlook Quick Steps can only move mail to ANOTHER OUTLOOK FOLDER, not to a
' disk folder — so a true one-click-to-disk uses this tiny VBA button instead.
' (CLASSIC Outlook only; the "new" Outlook has no VBA — there, drag/Save-As the
'  email as .eml into the synced _Inbox and let the scheduled script file it.)
'
' SETUP (once):
'   1. Outlook ▸ Alt+F11 (VBA editor) ▸ Project1 ▸ right-click ▸ Insert ▸ Module
'      ▸ paste everything below. Set INBOX to your synced library's _Inbox path.
